response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second, async I/O, high-performance data stores) and a strong testing and mentoring culture. However, the role's core minimum qualification is 8 years of C++, and the resume shows well under a year of professional C++ (National Instruments, ~7 months, plus a thesis project). His recent work is TypeScript/Node product engineering rather than cloud compute platform architecture, and there is no evidence of formally leading or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers on platform strategy. Strong engineer, wrong profile for this specific technical-lead role.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices at millions of HTTP requests/sec using async I/O, fibers, caching)
18+ years of broad engineering experience with a strong testing culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing: workshops, multi-day courses, conference talks, staff-level roles
concerns: Professional C++ experience is far below the 8-year minimum requirement (roughly 7 months, plus academic projects)
Recent experience is full-stack product work in TypeScript/Node, not cloud compute platform or infrastructure architecture ownership
No formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers or setting platform-wide technical strategy

key_factors.factor: Misalignment with cloud compute platform leadership scope
Low-latency distributed systems and testing expertise
